Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Referral Letter for clinical domain Maternal Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Manipuri (code=mni, script=Meitei). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Manipur, district=Thoubal.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Meitei for Manipuri.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 21
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Sports Goods Maker, Wood
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Manipur
- district: Thoubal
- first_language: Manipuri
- clinical_persona_summary: A 21-year-old female from Manipur, Thoubal district, works as a woodcraft maker with primary education and never married. She speaks Manipuri and follows a traditional Meitei Hindu lifestyle centered on craft and community. Her routine involves precise measurements and quality checks in her workshop.

Document format guide for Referral Letter (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-01]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|36]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Reason: <domain-appropriate clinical reason matching persona>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- REFERRAL_ID — Referral ID e.g. [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PASSPORT_NUMBER — Passport Number e.g. [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
- DRIVING_LICENCE — Driving Licence Number e.g. [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete ASHA Worker Note for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Maharashtra, district=Chandrapur.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Marathi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 65
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Chandrapur
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: Mr. Buddhaprakash Temghare, a 65-year-old male, is a retired homemaker from Chandrapur, Maharashtra, who is currently married and has primary education. He resides in an urban zone and speaks Marathi as his first language, with functional Hindi proficiency and modest English skills. His lifestyle includes mindful meditation, light yoga, and participation in community activities, with a preference for simple Marathi traditions and local cultural engagement.

Document format guide for ASHA Worker Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ASHA note — Village [[VILLAGE|...]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Beneficiary [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|26]] / [[GENDER|Female]]
ASHA: [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|...]] | Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Visit findings: <2-4 short sentences>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- ASHA_WORKER_NAME — ASHA Worker Name e.g. [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
- VILLAGE — Village e.g. [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- BPL_RATION_CARD — BPL / Ration Card Number e.g. [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
- VOTER_ID — Voter ID e.g. [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
- RELIGION — Religion e.g. [[RELIGION|Hindu]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]

  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete PHC Register Entry (Primary Health Centre) for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Oncology / Complex Chronic Care — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Maharashtra, district=Chandrapur.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Marathi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 65
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Chandrapur
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 65-year-old male from Chandrapur, Maharashtra, currently married, with primary education and an urban residence. His first language is Marathi, with functional Hindi, and he follows simple Marathi traditions. Clinically, he is a retired homemaker with a history of mindful meditation and low-impact activities like morning walks.

Document format guide for PHC Register Entry (Primary Health Centre) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
PHC register — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|PHC ...]] Village [[VILLAGE|...]] District [[DISTRICT|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|19]] [[GENDER|Female]] | brief entry line

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- VILLAGE — Village e.g. [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ASHA_WORKER_NAME — ASHA Worker Name e.g. [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
- BPL_RATION_CARD — BPL / Ration Card Number e.g. [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
- VOTER_ID — Voter ID e.g. [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

प्राथमिक आरोग्य केंद्र नोंदवही — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Primary Health Centre, Wadgaon]] गाव [[VILLAGE|Wadgaon]] जिल्हा [[DISTRICT|Chandrapur]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Patil]] [[AGE|65]] [[GENDER|Male]] [[VOTER_ID|MH441234567890]] [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] [[ABHA_ID|1234-5678-9012-3456]] | स्टेज III तोंडाच्या कर्करोगासाठी फॉलो-अप
रुग्ण हा 65 वर्षांचा पुरुष, निवृत्त गृहस्थ, विवाहित, प्राथमिक शिक्षण घेतलेला आहे. तो चंद्रपूरच्या शहरी भागात राहतो. तो बीपीएल कार्डधारक आहे [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-MH-44-1234567]] आणि त्याची आशा कार्यकर्ती [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Kamble]] आहे.
मुख्य तक्रार: एक आठवड्यापासून तोंडातील वेदना वाढणे आणि गिळण्यास त्रास होणे.
सध्याच्या आजाराचा इतिहास: श्री पाटील, ज्यांना दोन महिन्यांपूर्वी स्टेज III डाव्या गालाच्या श्लेष्मल त्वचेचा कर्करोग (buccal mucosa carcinoma) झाल्याचे निदान झाले आहे, ते सध्या केमोरेडिएशनवर आहेत. त्यांनी वेदना वाढल्याचे (7/10) आणि गिळताना वेदना (odynophagia) होत असल्याचे सांगितले, तसेच 3 किलो वजन कमी झाले आहे. सध्याची वेदनाशामक औषधे पुरेशी आराम देत नाहीत.
तपासणी: डाव्या गालाच्या श्लेष्मल त्वचेवर ग्रेड 3 म्युकोसायटिस आढळले. मानमध्ये गाठ जाणवत नाही. तोंडी आहार कमी आहे.
मूल्यमापन: केमोरेडिएशनवर असलेला स्टेज III तोंडाचा कर्करोग, ज्यामध्ये ग्रेड 3 म्युकोसायटिस आणि गिळताना वेदना (odynophagia) या गुंतागुंती आहेत.
योजना: 1. केमोरेडिएशन सुरू ठेवावे. 2. ब्रेकथ्रू पेनसाठी ट्रॅमॅडॉल 50mg लिहून द्यावे. 3. मऊ, उच्च-कॅलरी आहार आणि तोंडी स्वच्छतेबद्दल सल्ला द्यावा. 4. पोषण मूल्यमापन आणि संभाव्य फीडिंग ट्यूब बसवण्यासाठी जिल्हा रुग्णालयात पाठवावे. 5. एक आठवड्यात फॉलो-अप घ्यावा. घरभेटीच्या समर्थनासाठी आशा कार्यकर्ती सुनीता कंबळे यांना कळवावे.

Patient is a 65-year-old male, retired homemaker, married, with primary education. He resides in an urban area of Chandrapur. He is a BPL cardholder [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-MH-44-1234567]] and his ASHA worker is [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Kamble]].
CHIEF COMPLAINT: Increasing oral pain and difficulty swallowing for one week.
HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS: Mr. Patil, diagnosed with Stage III left buccal mucosa carcinoma two months ago, is on concurrent chemoradiation. He reports worsening pain (7/10) and odynophagia, with a 3 kg weight loss. Current analgesics provide inadequate relief.
EXAMINATION: Grade 3 mucositis noted on the left buccal mucosa. No palpable cervical nodes. Oral intake is poor.
ASSESSMENT: Stage III Oral Cancer on chemoradiation, complicated by Grade 3 mucositis and odynophagia.
PLAN: 1. Continue chemoradiation. 2. Prescribe Tramadol 50mg for breakthrough pain. 3. Counsel on soft, high-calorie diet and oral hygiene. 4. Refer to District Hospital for nutritional assessment and potential feeding tube placement. 5. Follow-up in one week. ASHA worker Sunita Kamble to be informed for home visit support.

professional_persona: Ankur Gain, a 54-year-old stock clerk with over three decades of inventory control expertise, they blend meticulous ledger accuracy and modern software fluency with a competitive drive to become a regional supervisor, while channeling their artistic flair into eye-catching product displays and community handicraft exhibitions.
linguistic_persona: Ankur Gain, fluent in Nepali, proficient in English for professional reading and communication, and conversational in Hindi for daily interactions, they deftly navigate Darjeeling’s multilingual market, switching languages to serve customers and coordinate with colleagues.
sports_persona: Ankur Gain, an avid trekker who joins weekend hikes in the Himalayas and follows cricket passionately, cheering for the Kolkata Knight Riders and supporting the local Darjeeling United football club, they stay fit through regular hill walks and occasional yoga sessions.
arts_persona: Ankur Gain, a passionate collector of Nepali folk music and tea-garden artwork, they regularly attend performances by singer Bimali Thapa, admire the watercolor paintings of Amit Joshi, and spend evenings sketching sunrise over tea estates in their notebook.
travel_persona: Ankur Gain, an adventure-seeking trekker who spends holidays exploring Himalayan trails in Darjeeling, Kalimpong and Sikkim, and dreams of a cultural immersion trip to Kathmandu and Bhutan, they plan carefully to balance modest budgets with a desire for authentic experiences.
culinary_persona: Ankur Gain, an enthusiastic home cook for family gatherings, they love preparing hearty Nepali dishes such as momo, sel roti, aloo dum, and thukpa, savor the tangy flavor of gundruk, and always serve meals with freshly brewed Darjeeling tea.
persona: Ankur Gain, a 54-year-old inventory veteran with a competitive edge, they blend meticulous stock-keeping with a love for Himalayan art, folk music, and trekking, while habitually juggling cash savings and occasional health neglect.
cultural_background: Ankur was born and raised in the bustling hill town of Darjeeling, West Bengal, where the Nepali‑speaking Gorkha community forms a core part of the social fabric. Growing up amidst tea gardens, colonial‑era architecture and a vibrant mix of Nepali, Indian and Tibetan influences, he inherited the customs of a Hindu Nepali family—celebrating festivals like Dashain and Tihar, partaking in local folk music and dance, and being familiar with the tea‑garden lifestyle. The urban setting, with its constant flow of tourists and diverse cultural events, has also exposed him to varied artistic expressions and modern ideas, feeding his curiosity for art and new experiences. His outgoing nature makes him a familiar face at community gatherings, while his competitive streak drives him to excel in his work.
linguistic_background: Ankur's mother tongue is Nepali; he is completely literate in Nepali, able to read, write and speak fluently. His formal education, a graduate degree in a non‑technical field, required proficiency in English, so he can understand and use English for reading academic material and workplace communication. Living and working in Darjeeling has given him conversational ability in Hindi, primarily for interacting with customers and colleagues, though he does not consider himself fully proficient. He has little exposure to Bengali despite residing in West Bengal, and he does not have any additional foreign language skills.
skills_and_expertise: Ankur has over three decades of experience as a stock clerk, giving him strong expertise in inventory control, stock auditing, and supply‑chain basics. He is adept at using manual ledgers as well as computer‑based inventory software, performs accurate data entry, and maintains meticulous records. His role requires effective customer interaction, product knowledge, and basic accounting for stock reconciliation. He possesses good organizational abilities, time‑management, and team coordination skills, and he can handle high‑pressure situations in busy retail environments. His curiosity about art also equips him with a basic understanding of local handicrafts and cultural products, which helps in presenting items attractively.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Inventory Management', 'Stock Auditing', 'Data Entry', 'Customer Service', 'Basic Accounting', 'MS Office', 'Team Coordination', 'Supply Chain Basics', 'Product Knowledge', 'Art and Handicraft Presentation']
hobbies_and_interests: Ankur enjoys participating in group activities such as local cultural festivals, community gatherings and trekking trips in the Himalayas. He has a keen interest in Nepali folk music and dance, often attending live performances. His love for art leads him to visit galleries, collect small paintings and handicrafts, and experiment with sketching scenic tea‑garden landscapes. He also likes playing cards with friends, watching classic Bollywood movies, and cooking traditional Nepali dishes for family gatherings.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Attending local cultural festivals', 'Group trekking trips in the Himalayas', 'Listening to Nepali folk music and dance performances', 'Visiting art galleries and collecting paintings/handicrafts', 'Sketching tea garden landscapes', 'Playing cards with friends', 'Watching classic Bollywood movies', 'Cooking traditional Nepali dishes for family']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Ankur aspires to move into a supervisory or managerial role within inventory and supply‑chain operations, aiming to oversee multiple stockrooms across the Darjeeling region. He intends to acquire formal certification in logistics management to complement his hands‑on experience. Additionally, he hopes to leverage his passion for art by organizing community exhibitions that showcase local Nepali handicrafts and tea‑garden cultural items, thereby marrying his professional expertise with his artistic interests. He also wishes to mentor younger staff, helping them develop work discipline and appreciation for cultural heritage.
persona_clinical_summary: A 54-year-old male stock clerk, currently married, with a graduate education working in an urban West Bengal district. Nepali is his first language, and he has a history of cash savings habits and occasional health neglect. His lifestyle includes a competitive drive for advancement and a love for Himalayan trekking.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Nepali (code=ne, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=West Bengal, district=Darjiling.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Nepali.

PLACEMENT (OPD header): First line MUST include BOTH [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] (facility name) AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] (facility code) — never substitute one for the other.
Put [[EMPLOYEE_ID|…]] on the registrar/clerk line and [[RELATIVE_NAME|…]] on the attendant/relative line. Include [[DOB|YYYY-MM-DD]] and [[OCCUPATION|…]] with demographics.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 54
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Stock Clerks, Other
- education_level: Graduate & above
- zone: Urban
- state: West Bengal
- district: Darjiling
- first_language: Nepali
- clinical_persona_summary: A 54-year-old male stock clerk, currently married, with a graduate education working in an urban West Bengal district. Nepali is his first language, and he has a history of cash savings habits and occasional health neglect. His lifestyle includes a competitive drive for advancement and a love for Himalayan trekking.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (adapt language/script; keep tags):
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital X]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-X-01]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] | DOB [[DOB|1992-03-14]] | Age: [[AGE|34]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. ...]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4412]] | District: [[DISTRICT|...]]
Chief complaint: <short clinical line matching assigned domain>

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- OCCUPATION — Occupation e.g. [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

बहिःबिरामी विभागको स्लिप | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Darjiling District Hospital]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DDH-WB-001]]
बिरामी: [[PATIENT_NAME|Kumar Rai]] | जन्म मिति [[DOB|1970-05-15]] | उमेर: [[AGE|54]] | लिङ्ग: [[GENDER|Male]]
व्यवसाय: [[OCCUPATION|Stock Clerks]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-001]] | डाक्टर: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sanjay Sharma]]
परिजन: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sunita Rai]] | फोन: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
दर्ता कर्मचारी ID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-DRJ-042]] | जिल्ला: [[DISTRICT|Darjiling]]
मुख्य गुनासो: २ दिनदेखि हल्का ज्वरो सहित दायाँ जाँघमा दुखाइ हुने सुजन

वर्तमान बिरामीको इतिहास: बिरामीले कामको क्रममा भारी बाकस उठाउँदा दायाँ जाँघमा अचानक सुजन सुरु भएको जानकारी दिएका छन्। दुखाइ तिखो छ, भित्री जाँघतिर फैलिएको छ। हल्का ज्वरो र भोक कम भएको सम्बन्धित छ। वमन वा बान्ता नभएको।

परीक्षणका निष्कर्षहरू: दायाँ इन्गुइनल क्षेत्रमा 5x4 cm को घटाउन सकिने सुजन देखिन्छ, छोएमा दुख्ने। खोकीको आवेग छैन। पेट नरम छ, आन्द्राको आवाज उपस्थित छ। अन्य कुनै असामान्यता फेला परेको छैन।

जाँचहरू: पूर्ण रक्त गणनाले हल्का ल्युकोसाइटोसिस देखाउँछ। पेटको अल्ट्रासाउन्डले दायाँ इन्गुइनल हर्निया पुष्टि गर्दछ।

निदान: प्रारम्भिक कैद सहित दायाँ इन्गुइनल हर्निया

योजना: शल्यक्रियाका लागि भर्ना गर्ने। NPO स्थिति, IV फ्लुइड्स, र प्रोफिल्याक्टिक एन्टिबायोटिक्स सहित शल्यक्रिया पूर्वको तयारी। आपत्कालीन हर्नियोर्राफीका लागि तालिका बनाउने।

अनुगमन: ७ दिनमा शल्यक्रिया पछिको समीक्षा। ४ हप्ताका लागि भारी उठाउनबाट बच्न सल्लाह दिने।

Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|Kumar Rai]] | DOB [[DOB|1970-05-15]] | Age: [[AGE|54]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Male]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Stock Clerks]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sanjay Sharma]]

Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-DRJ-042]] | District: [[DISTRICT|Darjiling]]
Chief complaint: Painful swelling in right groin for 2 days with mild fever

History of present illness: Patient reports sudden onset of right groin swelling while lifting heavy boxes at work. Pain is sharp, radiating to inner thigh. Associated with mild fever and decreased appetite. No nausea or vomiting.

Examination findings: Right inguinal region shows 5x4 cm reducible swelling, tender to touch. No cough impulse. Abdomen soft, bowel sounds present. No other abnormalities detected.

Investigations: Complete blood count shows mild leukocytosis. Ultrasound abdomen confirms right inguinal hernia.

Diagnosis: Right inguinal hernia with early incarceration

Plan: Admit for surgical repair. Pre-operative preparation including NPO status, IV fluids, and prophylactic antibiotics. Schedule for emergency herniorrhaphy.

Follow-up: Post-operative review in 7 days. Advise to avoid heavy lifting for 4 weeks.

Living and working in Darjeeling has given him conversational ability in Hindi, primarily for interacting with customers and colleagues, though he does not consider himself fully proficient. He has little exposure to Bengali despite residing in West Bengal, and he does not have any additional foreign language skills.
skills_and_expertise: Ankur has over three decades of experience as a stock clerk, giving him strong expertise in inventory control, stock auditing, and supply‑chain basics. He is adept at using manual ledgers as well as computer‑based inventory software, performs accurate data entry, and maintains meticulous records. His role requires effective customer interaction, product knowledge, and basic accounting for stock reconciliation. He possesses good organizational abilities, time‑management, and team coordination skills, and he can handle high‑pressure situations in busy retail environments. His curiosity about art also equips him with a basic understanding of local handicrafts and cultural products, which helps in presenting items attractively.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Inventory Management', 'Stock Auditing', 'Data Entry', 'Customer Service', 'Basic Accounting', 'MS Office', 'Team Coordination', 'Supply Chain Basics', 'Product Knowledge', 'Art and Handicraft Presentation']
hobbies_and_interests: Ankur enjoys participating in group activities such as local cultural festivals, community gatherings and trekking trips in the Himalayas. He has a keen interest in Nepali folk music and dance, often attending live performances. His love for art leads him to visit galleries, collect small paintings and handicrafts, and experiment with sketching scenic tea‑garden landscapes. He also likes playing cards with friends, watching classic Bollywood movies, and cooking traditional Nepali dishes for family gatherings.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Attending local cultural festivals', 'Group trekking trips in the Himalayas', 'Listening to Nepali folk music and dance performances', 'Visiting art galleries and collecting paintings/handicrafts', 'Sketching tea garden landscapes', 'Playing cards with friends', 'Watching classic Bollywood movies', 'Cooking traditional Nepali dishes for family']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Ankur aspires to move into a supervisory or managerial role within inventory and supply‑chain operations, aiming to oversee multiple stockrooms across the Darjeeling region. He intends to acquire formal certification in logistics management to complement his hands‑on experience. Additionally, he hopes to leverage his passion for art by organizing community exhibitions that showcase local Nepali handicrafts and tea‑garden cultural items, thereby marrying his professional expertise with his artistic interests. He also wishes to mentor younger staff, helping them develop work discipline and appreciation for cultural heritage.

Nepali is his first language, and he is employed in the stock control sector. His lifestyle includes a love for trekking and local folk music, with a noted tendency for occasional health neglect.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]

  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]

  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]

  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Surgical / Operative Note for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Nepali (code=ne, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Oncology / Complex Chronic Care — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=West Bengal, district=Darjiling.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Nepali.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 54
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Stock Clerks, Other
- education_level: Graduate & above
- zone: Urban
- state: West Bengal
- district: Darjiling
- first_language: Nepali
- clinical_persona_summary: A 54-year-old male stock clerk, currently married, with a graduate education working in an urban setting in West Bengal. Nepali is his first language, and he is employed in the stock control sector. His lifestyle includes a love for trekking and local folk music, with a noted tendency for occasional health neglect.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|50]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Procedure / findings: MUST describe an actual procedure/operation matching the
assigned clinical domain (not a psychiatry admission note). Keep relative names
gender-plausible.

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

शल्यक्रिया प्रतिवेदन — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|सिलिगुडी सुपर स्पेसालिटी हस्पिटल]] प्रवेश [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]] वार्ड [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|रमेश शर्मा]] [[AGE|54]] [[GENDER|Male]] शल्यचिकित्सक [[DOCTOR_NAME|डा. आनन्द घिमिरे]]
प्रक्रिया / निष्कर्ष:
बिरामीमा दायाँ माथिल्लो चतुर्थांशमा 4cm को गाँठो देखिएको थियो, इमेजिङको आधारमा यो ग्यास्ट्रोइन्टेस्टाइनल स्ट्रोमल ट्युमर हुन सक्ने आशङ्का गरिएको थियो। शल्यक्रिया पूर्वको बायोप्सीले GIST पुष्टि भयो। शल्यक्रिया भित्रका निष्कर्षहरूले पेटको ठुलो वक्रताबाट उत्पन्न भएको राम्ररी घेरिएको ट्युमर देखायो, मेटास्टेसिसको कुनै प्रमाण थिएन। ल्याप्रोस्कोपिक विधि प्रयोग गरेर ट्युमरलाई नेगेटिभ मार्जिनका साथ निकालियो। नमुनाको नाप 4.2 x 3.8 x 2.5 cm थियो। शल्यक्रिया पछिको अवस्था कुनै जटिलता बिनाको थियो। बिरामीले नरम आहार राम्ररी सहन गरे। विकृतिविज्ञानले स्पष्ट मार्जिनका साथ पूर्ण निष्कासन पुष्टि भयो। अन्तिम विकृतिविज्ञान प्रतिवेदनको आधारमा सहायक उपचार विचार गरिनेछ। [[BED_NUMBER|12]] [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] [[RELATIVE_NAME|श्रीमती उमा शर्मा]] [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
generated_text_en:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|North Bengal Medical College and Hospital]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Kumar Sharma]] [[AGE|54]] [[GENDER|Male]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Thapa]]
Procedure / findings: Patient presented with a 4cm mass in the right upper quadrant, suspected to be a gastrointestinal stromal tumor based on imaging. Preoperative biopsy confirmed GIST. Intraoperative findings revealed a well-circumscribed tumor arising from the greater curvature of the stomach, without evidence of metastasis. The tumor was resected with negative margins using laparoscopic approach. The specimen measured 4.2 x 3.8 x 2.5 cm. Postoperative course was uneventful. Patient tolerated soft diet well. Pathology confirmed complete resection with clear margins. Adjuvant therapy to be considered based on final pathology report. [[BED_NUMBER|12]] [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sunita Sharma]] [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
judge_reasoning: Nepali Devanagari prose, valid allow-listed entities, male/54/GIST fit, concise operative note, no script or domain issues.

professional_persona: Kishor Maharana is a 35‑year‑old master craftsman specializing in hand‑crafted footwear, blending traditional leather stitching with modest design tweaks, and they manage their workshop with diligent bookkeeping, reliable customer service, and a practical curiosity that drives them to explore new tools and expand into nearby towns and online platforms.
linguistic_persona: Kishor Maharana primarily communicates in Odia, comfortably reading and writing in it, and they possess functional Hindi for official dealings while their exposure to English is limited to trade‑specific jargon and a few borrowed terms.
sports_persona: Kishor Maharana follows cricket passionately on the radio, roots for Chennai Super Kings in the IPL, and joins occasional village‑level cricket matches and kabaddi games, using them as a lively outlet for fitness and camaraderie.
arts_persona: Kishor Maharana revels in Odia folk songs and bhajans, often tapping the dholak to tunes by Akshaya Mohanty and Prafulla Kar, while also enjoying Bollywood blockbusters starring Amitabh Bachchan and Shah Rukh Khan, and they occasionally attend Odissi dance showcases during village festivals.
travel_persona: Kishor Maharana prefers budget bus trips to nearby Puri for Rath Yatra and Bhubaneswar for market deals, occasionally journeys to Kolkata by train, and dreams of a pilgrimage to Delhi’s historic forts, planning each outing through word‑of‑mouth and modest mobile apps.
culinary_persona: Kishor Maharana enjoys cooking traditional Odia fare like dalma, chakuli pitha, and roti, seasoning with mustard seeds, ginger and coconut, and they love serving sweet treats such as chhena poda and rasgulla to family and neighbours during festivals.
persona: Kishor Maharana is a practical yet curious village shoemaker who balances tradition with modest innovation, values community harmony above all, and quietly juggles workshop duties, family hopes, and a habit of lingering over paperwork.
cultural_background: Born and raised in the rural village of Nabarangapur, Odisha, Kishor Maharana hails from a traditional Odia artisan community where hand-crafted footwear has been passed down through generations. His cultural life revolves around village festivals like Rath Yatra, Nuakhai and local worship of village deities, as well as communal storytelling and folk music. While deeply rooted in these customs, his innate curiosity leads him to experiment with modest design innovations, reflecting a practical blend of tradition and modernity.
linguistic_background: Kishor’s first language is Odia, which he speaks, reads and writes with confidence, allowing him to manage day‑to‑day transactions, follow local news and keep records for his workshop. He has a basic functional understanding of Hindi, mainly for official communication and occasional market dealings, but he does not read or write Hindi fluently. His schooling only went up to primary level, so his exposure to English is minimal, limited to a few borrowed terms used in the trade.
skills_and_expertise: Kishor is skilled in hand‑crafted shoe making, including leather cutting, pattern drafting, hand stitching, and finishing. He can design simple styles that suit local preferences and repair worn footwear efficiently. His practical mindset ensures consistent quality, while his curiosity pushes him to experiment with modest design tweaks. He also manages the day‑to‑day operations of his small workshop, handling inventory, basic bookkeeping, and direct customer service with a cooperative and reliable approach.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['hand-crafted shoe making', 'leather cutting', 'pattern drafting', 'hand stitching', 'shoe repair', 'basic workshop management', 'inventory tracking', 'customer service']
hobbies_and_interests: In his free time, Kishor enjoys listening to Odia folk songs and bhajans, occasionally playing the dholak during village gatherings. He follows cricket matches on the radio and likes to watch short tutorial videos on shoe design on his mobile phone. Gardening, especially growing seasonal vegetables, offers him quiet relaxation, while cooking traditional Odia dishes such as dalma and roti lets him share meals with family and neighbours. He also participates in local fairs and helps organize community events.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['listening to Odia folk music', 'playing dholak', 'watching cricket', 'viewing shoe design videos', 'gardening', 'cooking traditional Odia dishes', 'participating in village fairs', 'helping organize community events']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Kishor aims to grow his modest shoe‑making workshop into a more organized small enterprise that can supply nearby towns and potentially tap online platforms. He plans to learn basic modern tailoring tools, improve design variety, and train a couple of apprentices from his village. By increasing his income, he hopes to provide higher‑level education for his children and secure a comfortable retirement for himself, all while preserving the traditional craft and maintaining the harmonious village life he values.
occupation: Shoe Maker and Related Workers, Other
persona_clinical_summary: A 35-year-old male from Nabarangapur, Odisha, works as a shoemaker with primary education and is married. He speaks Odia as his first language and resides in a rural area. His lifestyle includes managing workshop duties and community responsibilities.

- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):

ମୁଖ୍ୟ ଅଭିଯୋଗ: ଗତ ତିନି ମାସ ଧରି ଲଗାତାର ଚିନ୍ତା ଏବଂ ଶୋଇବାରେ ଅସୁବିଧା ହେଉଛି।

ବର୍ତ୍ତମାନର ଅସୁସ୍ଥତାର ଇତିହାସ:
ରୋଗୀ, ଜଣେ 35 ବର୍ଷର ପୁରୁଷ ମୋଚି, ଗତ ତିନି ମାସ ଧରି ବଢୁଥିବା ଚିନ୍ତା ଏବଂ ଚାପ ବିଷୟରେ କହୁଛନ୍ତି। ସେ ନିଜର ଆର୍ଥିକ ପରିସ୍ଥିତି ଏବଂ ପାରିବାରିକ ଦାୟିତ୍ୱ ବିଷୟରେ ଲଗାତାର ଚିନ୍ତା କରୁଛନ୍ତି। ସେ ଶୋଇବାରେ ଅସୁବିଧା, ରାତିରେ ବାରମ୍ବାର ଉଠିବା ଏବଂ ସକାଳ ଶୀଘ୍ର ଉଠିବା ବିଷୟରେ ସ୍ୱୀକାର କରୁଛନ୍ତି। ସେ ହୃଦସ୍ପନ୍ଦନ ବଢିବା, କ୍ଲାନ୍ତି ଏବଂ କାମରେ ଧ୍ୟାନ ଦେବାରେ ଅସୁବିଧା ବିଷୟରେ ମଧ୍ୟ କହୁଛନ୍ତି। ରୋଗୀ କୌଣସି ଆତ୍ମହତ୍ୟା ଚିନ୍ତା କିମ୍ବା ଭ୍ରମ ବିଷୟରେ ମନା କରୁଛନ୍ତି। ତାଙ୍କର ଭୋକ କମିଯାଇଛି ଏବଂ ସେ ପ୍ରାୟ 5 kg ଓଜନ ହରାଇଛନ୍ତି।

ମୂଲ୍ୟାଙ୍କନ:
ରୋଗୀଙ୍କଠାରେ ଜେନେରାଲାଇଜଡ୍ ଆଙ୍ଗଜାଇଟି ଡିସଅର୍ଡର (GAD) ସହିତ ଇନସୋମନିଆ ଲକ୍ଷଣ ଦେଖାଯାଉଛି।

ଯୋଜନା:
1. ସକାଳ ସମୟରେ ଦିନକୁ ଥରେ 50mg Sertraline ଲେଖି ଦିଆଯାଇଛି।
2. ରିଲାକ୍ସେସନ୍ ଟେକ୍ନିକ୍ ଏବଂ ଗଭୀର ଶ୍ୱାସକ୍ରିୟା ବ୍ୟାୟାମ କରିବାକୁ ପରାମର୍ଶ ଦିଆଯାଇଛି।
3. ନିୟମିତ ନିଦ୍ରା ସ୍ୱଚ୍ଛତାର ଗୁରୁତ୍ୱ ବିଷୟରେ ପରାମର୍ଶ ଦିଆଯାଇଛି।
4. ସାପ୍ତାହିକ ସେସନ୍ ପାଇଁ ହସ୍ପିଟାଲର କାଉନସେଲିଂ ସାଇକୋଲୋଜିଷ୍ଟଙ୍କ ପାଖକୁ ପଠାଯାଇଛି।
5. ଦୁଇ ସପ୍ତାହ ପରେ ଫଲୋ-ଅପ୍ ଆପଏଣ୍ଟମେଣ୍ଟ୍ ସ୍ଥିର କରାଯାଇଛି।
6. ଲକ୍ଷଣ ବଢିଲେ କିମ୍ବା ଆତ୍ମହତ୍ୟା ଚିନ୍ତା ଆସିଲେ ତୁରନ୍ତ ସାହାଯ୍ୟ ମାଗିବାକୁ ରୋଗୀଙ୍କୁ ପରାମର୍ଶ ଦିଆଯାଇଛି।
